summary: Phase 4 - Room Management + Room Assignment. Backend: re-ran the existing pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_rooms.py against the live preview (REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL) - 11/11 passed in 9.17s, covering CRUD, case-insensitive name uniqueness, hard-delete blocked when history exists, assign/change/clear-room with appointment_room_history rows, single-occupancy 409, force=true requires reason (400 without, 200 with audit forced=true), inactive-room rejection, terminal-appointment rejection, and patient-portal denial. Frontend: drove the live UI with admin@ccms.app - /settings/rooms (RoomsManagerPage) renders, step-up reauth modal triggers as expected and was satisfied via 'Use password instead' (PIN -> password fallback), New room create flow succeeded (TESTRoom-799025 appeared in the list immediately, total 23 rooms now seeded). RoomAssignmentControl source has all the required testids (room-assignment-control, room-current-*, room-override-*, room-none-*, room-assign-btn-*, room-picker-*, room-picker-option-*, room-picker-empty-*, room-return-waiting-btn-*, room-clear-btn-*). Patient role denial confirmed: patient@ccms.app -> /settings/rooms redirects to '/', and nav-rooms entry is NOT in the patient sidebar. DayView 'Room *' line is wired but not currently displayed because there are no checked-in appointments today carrying a current_room_name (consistent with iter-55 state where the only seeded appt was driven through to checked_out). Same reason the FlowBoard RoomAssignmentControl count was 0 in the active session - no appointments are presently in waiting_room/roomed/ready_for_provider/in_progress/ready_for_checkout columns; the control is conditionally rendered per row, so empty state == 0 controls. To exercise the FlowBoard control end-to-end the main agent would need to seed a today-checked-in appointment.

critical_code_review_comments: rooms/router.py: scoped_filter return is checked for '__deny__' before insert - OK. _validate_location lets platform admins through but the location_id is still required to exist - good defensive shape.

scheduling/rooms.py assign_or_change_room: cross-location check `current.get('location_id') != room['location_id']` is only enforced when the appointment has a location_id; appointments without location_id are silently allowed to bind to any room. Low risk in practice (locations are required at booking) but worth tightening to a hard 400 if location_id is missing.

scheduling/rooms.py: TERMINAL set lists both 'canceled' and 'cancelled' - good defensive coverage but indicates inconsistent spelling elsewhere in the codebase; consider normalising on one variant in models.
RoomAssignmentControl.jsx: all interactive elements correctly carry data-testid - matches review-request testid contract (room-current-*, room-override-*, room-none-*, room-picker-option-*, etc.).
RoomsManagerPage.jsx ~360 lines - well within size limits; CRUD list handles deactivate vs delete separately as designed.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of testable Phase-4 UI surfaces (rooms CRUD, reauth flow, patient denial); FlowBoard / DayView room badges not visually exercised because no qualifying appointment exists today

test_credentials: admin@ccms.app / Admin@ComplianceClinic1 (step-up: 'Use password instead' -> same password). patient@ccms.app / Patient@ComplianceClinic1 for denial.
seed_data_creation: Created one room via UI (name TESTRoom-799025, type=exam) at the admin's primary location. Test_rooms.py also creates several TEST rooms each run (Room-<hex>, Renamed <hex>, Consult <hex>) - they accumulate but are scoped to the test tenant/location and never assigned outside the test flow.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase-4 backend is fully green (test_rooms.py 11/11). UI surfaces verified: /settings/rooms create+list, step-up reauth, patient role denial. To finish the visual loop on FlowBoard/DayView the next agent should: (1) /scheduling day view -> open any today appointment in BookDialog -> Check-in (step-up via password). (2) Open /scheduling/flow-board -> the row in Waiting Room column will show RoomAssignmentControl with 'No room' badge and 'Assign' button. (3) Click room-assign-btn-* -> picker popover lists active rooms with 'Occupied' badge per room currently held; pick one -> badge becomes 'Room: X'. (4) Trigger an override by trying the same room from a second checked-in appointment -> 409, then retry with reason -> 200 + 'Override' pill renders. (5) Confirm DayView card shows 'Room * <name>' line. Backend assert path already covered in test_rooms.py.
